# @zakkster/lite-headless/notification-center

Persistent list of past notifications with mark-read, filtering, and
clear-all. The "bell icon with unread badge" UX, used in nearly every
admin dashboard.

Distinct from `createToaster`:

|              | toast              | notification-center  |
|--------------|--------------------|----------------------|
| lifetime     | ephemeral (N sec)  | persistent           |
| dismissal    | auto + click       | manual mark-read     |
| stack        | viewport overlay   | collapsible panel    |
| unread state | n/a                | first-class          |
| filtering    | n/a                | by kind, by read     |

Common pattern: toast.show() fires + same payload pushed into the
notification center. The center keeps the user's history.

## API

```js
import { createNotificationCenter } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/notification-center";

const nc = createNotificationCenter({
    defaultNotifications: [
        { id: "n1", title: "Server restarted", kind: "info",    timestamp: Date.now() - 60000 },
        { id: "n2", title: "Build passed",      kind: "success", timestamp: Date.now() - 1000 },
    ],
    maxItems: 100,
    defaultFilter: null,
    onChange:      (notifications) => {},
    onMarkRead:    (id) => {},
    onMarkAllRead: () => {},
    onClear:       (id) => {},
    onClearAll:    () => {},
});
```

## Notification shape

```js
{
    id:        string,
    title:     string,
    body:      string,     // optional
    kind:      "info" | "success" | "warning" | "error" | "system",
    timestamp: Date | number,    // ms epoch or Date; default = now
    read:      boolean,    // default false
    meta:      {},         // consumer-defined
}
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
nc.notifications()    // NotificationDef[] newest-first
nc.unreadCount()      // number
nc.visible()          // NotificationDef[] passing the current filter
nc.filter()           // { kind?, read?: boolean } | null
```

## Queries

```js
nc.getNotification(id)
```

## Mutations

```js
nc.add(notification)            // prepends; dedup by id (newer replaces)
nc.remove(id)
nc.update(id, partial)
nc.setNotifications(arr)
nc.markRead(id) / .markUnread(id) / .markAllRead()
nc.clearAll() / .clearByKind(kind) / .clearRead()
nc.setFilter({ kind, read }) / .clearFilter()
```

## Attach methods

```js
nc.attachRoot(el)                 // role=region; data-nc-unread + data-nc-has-unread
nc.attachItem(el, id)             // per-row paint + click-marks-read
nc.attachUnreadBadge(el)          // textContent = unread count; "99+" clamp
nc.attachClearAllButton(el)
nc.attachMarkAllReadButton(el)
```

## Painted attributes

### Root (attachRoot)
- `role="region"`, `aria-label="Notifications"`
- `data-nc-unread="<count>"`
- `data-nc-has-unread` if count > 0

### Items (attachItem)
- `role="listitem"`, `tabindex="0"`
- `data-nc-id="<id>"`
- `data-nc-kind="<kind>"`
- `data-nc-read` (read) / `data-nc-unread` (unread)
- `data-nc-missing` if the notification no longer exists (consumer
  should remove the element)

### Unread badge (attachUnreadBadge)
- `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`
- `data-nc-hidden` if count = 0 (consumer CSS hides via `[data-nc-hidden]`)

## Custom element

```html
<lite-notification-center max-items="100">
    <header>
        <h2>Notifications</h2>
        <span data-nc-badge></span>
        <button data-nc-mark-all-read>Mark all read</button>
        <button data-nc-clear-all>Clear all</button>
    </header>
    <div data-nc-list role="list">
        <article data-nc-id="n1" data-nc-title="Server restarted" data-nc-kind="info"></article>
        <article data-nc-id="n2" data-nc-title="Build passed"     data-nc-kind="success"></article>
    </div>
</lite-notification-center>
```

### Custom element host API

```js
host.notifications              // accessor: NotificationDef[]
host.unreadCount                // accessor: number
host.visible                    // accessor: NotificationDef[]
host.add(n) / .remove(id) / .update(id, partial)
host.markRead(id) / .markUnread(id) / .markAllRead()
host.clearAll() / .clearByKind(kind) / .clearRead()
host.setFilter(f) / .clearFilter()
host._notificationCenterInstance
```

### Custom element events

- `ncchange` `{ detail: { notifications } }` -- fires after any mutation

## Composition

- **`lite-popover`**: bell icon trigger + popover containing the center.
- **`lite-toast`**: pair toast.show() with nc.add() for live-feedback +
  history.
- **`lite-tabs`**: filter tabs (All / Unread / Errors) by calling
  setFilter on tab change.
- **`lite-skeleton`**: skeleton items while loading from server.

## Notes

- Notifications are stored newest-first. The `notifications()` and
  `visible()` accessors return arrays in that order; consumers can
  iterate them directly to render rows.
- `maxItems` is enforced on every `add()` and `setNotifications()` call.
  When the cap is hit, the OLDEST entries are evicted. To trim the
  newer end instead, manage the list yourself with `setNotifications`.
- The primitive doesn't fire its own toast — pairing with `createToaster`
  is the consumer's job. Typical pattern: a thin `notify()` helper
  calls both `nc.add(payload)` and `toaster.show(payload)`.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
